pi_items.verified_value: True - OpenSIS is confirmed to have attendance tracking capabilities
pi_items.source: Verified - OpenSIS documentation shows attendance tracking feature (https://www.opensis.com/feature-attendance); verifier.py queries attendance table (lines 36-52); setup grants permissions for 'attendance/TakeAttendance.php' in direct_db_setup.sh line 136

privileged_info_summary: The record_attendance task operates on OpenSIS v9.2, a real open-source Student Information System (GitHub: OS4ED/openSIS-Classic). The test student is named 'Sample Student' (DOB: 2005-05-15, Grade: 10) and is created synthetically during task setup. The OpenSIS database uses MySQL with credentials: user 'opensis_user' and password 'opensis_password_123'. The admin login username is 'admin' with password 'Admin@123' (NOTE: the task description incorrectly states the password as 'admin123' in lowercase, but the actual configured password is 'Admin@123' with capital A). The system runs on a LAMP stack (Apache 2.4+, MariaDB 10.4+, PHP 8.x) and includes full attendance tracking functionality. All data is synthetic - the student record is created during task initialization.
